name: google-contacts
description: Search, read, create, and update the authenticated user's Google Contacts via the People API

tools.name: List Contacts
tools.description: List the authenticated user's contacts (connections) with the requested person fields

personFields.description: Comma-separated person fields to return, e.g. names,emailAddresses,phoneNumbers,organizations

pageSize.description: Number of contacts to return per page (max 1000)

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous response for pagination

sortOrder.description: Sort order: LAST_MODIFIED_ASCENDING, LAST_MODIFIED_DESCENDING, FIRST_NAME_ASCENDING, or LAST_NAME_ASCENDING

tools.name: Search Contacts
tools.description: Search the user's contacts by prefix match on names, nicknames, email addresses, and phone numbers. For best results send a warmup request with an empty query first, then search.

query.description: Plain-text search query matched against prefix phrases (use an empty string for a cache warmup request)

readMask.description: Comma-separated person fields to return, e.g. names,emailAddresses,phoneNumbers

tools.name: Get Contact
tools.description: Get a single contact by person ID, including the etag needed for updates

personId.description: Person ID — the part after 'people/' in the contact's resourceName (e.g. for resourceName 'people/c123' pass 'c123'); use 'me' for the authenticated user

personFields.description: Comma-separated person fields to return; include metadata to retrieve the etag and sources required for updates

tools.name: List Contact Groups
tools.description: List the user's contact groups (labels), including system groups and member counts, e.g. to organize or filter contacts

pageSize.description: Number of groups to return per page (1-1000, defaults to 30)

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous response for pagination

groupFields.description: Comma-separated group fields to return; defaults to metadata,groupType,memberCount,name

tools.name: Create Contact
tools.description: Create a new contact in the user's Google Contacts (requires the full contacts scope)

personFields.description: Comma-separated person fields to return on the created contact

names.description: Array with a single {givenName, familyName} object (singleton field — at most one entry)

addresses.description: Array of postal address objects, e.g. {formattedValue, type}

tools.name: Update Contact
tools.description: Update an existing contact (requires the full contacts scope). You must pass updatePersonFields naming the fields you are changing, and the body must include the contact's current etag (fetch it with Get Contact including metadata first); a stale etag fails with a 400 failedPrecondition error.

personId.description: Person ID — the part after 'people/' in the contact's resourceName (e.g. for resourceName 'people/c123' pass 'c123')

updatePersonFields.description: Comma-separated field mask of the person fields being updated, e.g. names,emailAddresses,phoneNumbers

personFields.description: Comma-separated person fields to return on the updated contact

etag.description: Current etag of the contact, from a preceding Get Contact call

metadata.description: Person metadata copied from Get Contact (must include sources, or the API returns a 400 error)

names.description: Updated names array with a single {givenName, familyName} object (include 'names' in updatePersonFields)

emailAddresses.description: Updated array of {value, type} email objects (include 'emailAddresses' in updatePersonFields)

phoneNumbers.description: Updated array of {value, type} phone objects (include 'phoneNumbers' in updatePersonFields)

organizations.description: Updated array of {name, title} organization objects (include 'organizations' in updatePersonFields)

prompts.title: Find a contact
prompts.prompt: Search my Google Contacts for a person and show their email address and phone number.

prompts.title: Add a new contact
prompts.prompt: Create a new contact in my Google Contacts with name, email, and phone number.

prompts.title: Update contact details
prompts.prompt: Find a contact and update their email address or phone number in Google Contacts.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Google Contacts setup

steps.title: Create or reuse a Google Cloud project
steps.description: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and create a project, or reuse the project that already hosts your shared Google OAuth app. Any free Google account works for testing — add a few contacts at https://contacts.google.com to have data to query.

steps.title: Enable the People API
steps.description: Open https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/people.googleapis.com, select your project, and click Enable. Google Contacts data is served by the People API.

steps.title: Configure the OAuth consent screen and client
steps.description: Under APIs & Services > Credentials, create (or reuse) an OAuth 2.0 Client ID of type Web application and add your app's redirect URI ending in /api/auth/google-contacts/callback. The same Google OAuth client can be shared across all Google connectors.

steps.title: Set environment variables
steps.description: Set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET from the OAuth client you created. These are shared with other Google integrations.

notes: The default scope is read-only (contacts.readonly); creating or updating contacts requires granting the optional https://www.googleapis.com/auth/contacts scope.
Contact resource names look like 'people/c123'; tools take just the ID portion after 'people/' as the personId parameter.
Updates are etag-guarded: fetch the contact (including metadata) first and send its etag and metadata.sources back, plus an updatePersonFields mask naming the changed fields.
Search uses a prefix-match cache; send a warmup search with an empty query before the real query for fresh results.
